evals.expected_output: Spec derived entirely from the approved PRD. Contains all three test tiers. Acceptance criteria split into Functional/Unit/Integration/E2E sections. An agent reading only this spec could execute without questions.
assertions: spec.md written to .orchestra/work/core-logic/spec.md
spec.md frontmatter has status: draft
spec.md contains a ## Objective section restated from PRD
spec.md contains a ## Approach section with numbered steps
spec.md contains a ## Testing Strategy section
spec.md contains a ### Unit Tests subsection with named test files and covered behaviors
spec.md contains a ### Integration Tests subsection
spec.md integration tests state they hit real boundaries — no mocks at the seam
spec.md contains a ### E2E Tests subsection
spec.md contains a ## Deliverables table with concrete file paths
spec.md contains a ## Acceptance Criteria section
spec.md acceptance criteria split into ### Functional, ### Unit, ### Integration, ### E2E subsections
spec.md contains a ## Risks section with at least one risk and mitigation
Every PRD success criterion maps to at least one acceptance criterion in spec.md
spec.md approach steps are ordered by dependency — no step requires output from a later step
Skill reads the PRD before generating — does not ask user to re-explain the objective

evals.prompt: Write a spec for a work item that touches an external API
evals.expected_output: Integration tests section explicitly requires hitting the real API endpoint. Mocking the external boundary is called out as insufficient.
assertions: spec.md integration tests explicitly state they hit the real external API
spec.md does not permit mocking the external API in integration tests
spec.md includes at least one integration test that verifies real API response shape
spec.md ## Risks section includes risks related to the external API (rate limits, auth, schema drift)
